---
title: "Chapter 2: Advanced Topics"
format:
  html: default
  revealjs:
    output-file: chapter2-slides.html
  pdf:
    output-file: chapter2-handout.pdf
  docx:
    output-file: chapter2.docx
---

This is the second chapter. Continue building your website with more chapters.

# Mathematical Equations

You can include mathematical equations using LaTeX syntax:

Inline equation: $E = mc^2$

Display equation:

$$
\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi}
$$ {#eq-gaussian-integral}

Direct link to the equation anchor:
See @eq-gaussian-integral.

# Tables

| Column 1 | Column 2 | Column 3 |
|----------|----------|----------|
| Data 1   | Data 2   | Data 3   |
| Data 4   | Data 5   | Data 6   |

: Example table caption {#tbl-example}

# Figures

You can include images by placing them in the `images/` directory:

```markdown
![Example image caption](images/your-image.png){#fig-example}
```

Then reference the figure in text using `@fig-example`.

# Callouts

::: {.callout-note}
This is a note callout. Use it to highlight important information.
:::

::: {.callout-tip}
This is a tip callout. Share helpful suggestions with your readers.
:::

::: {.callout-warning}
This is a warning callout. Alert readers to potential issues.
:::

::: {.callout-important}
This is an important callout. Emphasize critical information.
:::

# Theorem-like Divs

::: {#lem-sample .lem}
This is a sample lemma-style div using the standard class name `lem`.
:::

::: {#exm-sample .exm}
This is a sample example-style div using the standard class name `exm`.
:::

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
